Observed keep-list from a pin llama-quantize NVFP4 run: IMPOSSIBLE and MISSING. The pin has no LLAMA_FTYPE_MOSTLY_NVFP4 default_type case (llama-quant.cpp:792-833) and throws invalid-output-file-type at 866-868; refusal_receipt.md was also absent from the bundle. Keep rules here are the extracted Python-conversion rules (base.py:697-711), the only path that produces NVFP4 GGUFs.

Kept embeddings (token_embd.weight, per_layer_token_embd.weight) are projected at their CURRENT Q8_0 bytes (3,707,764,736 B combined). The extraction says the conversion path keeps embeddings at BF16/F16/F32 (or FP8->dequant->Q8_0/F16); if a converter kept them at BF16 instead, they would cost 6,979,321,856 B and the total would be ~3.27 GB LARGER. Q8_0-keep is the favorable, receipt-cited floor.

per-tensor .scale/.input_scale sidecars counted as 2x F32 {1} = 8 B per repacked weight (conversion/base.py:685-686; TENSOR_NOT_REQUIRED per llama-model.cpp:1317-1477); this model has no experts, so no {n_expert} per-expert scales.

No NVFP4 candidate violates K%64: the only K%64 violations are the 42 blk.*.layer_output_scale.weight F32 scalars (K=1), which are never repacked; NVFP4 has NO K%64 fallback (llama-quant.cpp:362-408 default throws at :391).

The 6 GB verdict uses a STATED ASSUMPTION for KV/workspace (512 MiB, optimistic floor 256 MiB), not a measurement; Camelid KV default is f32, and Windows desktop/driver VRAM overhead is additionally unaccounted. Phase 2 must measure.

lm_head is tied (has_output_weight=false, lm_head_tied_inferred=true): no output.weight tensor exists to project; tied NVFP4 output would require no LM-head sidecar scales (llama-model.cpp:1472-1477) — keeping token_embd at Q8_0 sidesteps this.

### 6 GB residency check (RTX 3060 Laptop, 6144 MiB VRAM — hw_probe.json)
- Projected resident weights: 5917.1 MiB -> headroom before KV/workspace: 226.9 MiB.
- Stated allowance ASSUMPTION (not measured): 512 MiB for KV cache + compute workspace + CUDA context at moderate context; even an optimistic 256 MiB floor is shown.
- weights + 256 MiB = 6173.1 MiB vs 6144 MiB -> DOES NOT FIT.
- weights + 512 MiB = 6429.1 MiB vs 6144 MiB -> DOES NOT FIT.
